themes.exampleQuote: Shout out to Bradley who made it so easy for me to schedule our upcoming event.
This brewery has a lot of IPAs: that is a positive for me.
QR codes, then table service, and now suddenly you have to order at the bar, with no clear communication.
We had a great time in the party room right in front of the open garage door on a pleasant weekend afternoon.
Absolutely love the lights and the live edge tables.

notable_quote: The key, I WILL BE HERE AGAIN.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers keep coming back for the deep tap list, the party-friendly footprint, and a floor manager who shows up by name in four separate reviews; the one friction point is inconsistent ordering protocol that cost at least one regular.
quarter_narrative: Twenty-one Pittsburgh creators posted about Hop Farm this quarter, generating just under 171,000 TikTok plays, and none of that traffic runs through an account Hop Farm controls, because the brewery isn't on TikTok at all. The Instagram account is genuinely active, 12 posts and 4 reels in the last 30 days, but a 0.47 percent engagement rate means the audience isn't moving the way the creator coverage suggests it could. In the Pittsburgh Bars family, Hidden Harbor holds the top spot on the strength of social momentum; Hop Farm sits at number two with the stronger creator footprint and the stronger review depth, a gap that closes if the TikTok signal gets captured instead of donated to other people's channels.
tldr_read: Strong TikTok creator coverage and a near-perfect review rating, but no TikTok account to catch any of it. Ones to Watch, number one in Pittsburgh Breweries.
tldr_meaning: The data shows a business that the city is actively choosing to film and recommend, but whose own digital presence stops at Instagram, leaving the highest-reach channel entirely in other people's hands.

diagnosis_pullquote.line: Number One Brewery, Built On 171,000 TikTok Plays It Doesn't Own.

playbook.headline: Claim the TikTok ground others built
playbook.action: Open a Hop Farm TikTok account and repost the top creator clips, starting with the 51,500-play video from @stevenjamesyuhasz.

playbook.headline: Fix the front-door friction in reviews
playbook.action: Address the ordering system complaint publicly and directly, one unresolved negative review in 298 is manageable, but the pattern it names is a repeat-visit killer.

playbook.headline: Convert Instagram reels into a visual catalog
playbook.action: With 364 Google photos already up, push reel content that shows the live edge interior, the party room, and the tap list; the 0.47 percent engagement rate says the current mix isn't doing it.
